title: STANDARD FOR DRIED FLORAL PARTS - SAFFRON

sections.section: STANDARD FOR DRIED FLORAL PARTS - SAFFRON
sections.summary: Document title: Standard for dried floral parts - saffron (informative heading only).
Document identification: CXS 351-2022, adopted in 2022 (document code/metadata only).

Applies to dried or dehydrated plant products used as spices (saffron) offered for direct consumption, use as a food ingredient, or repackaging, and explicitly excludes products intended for industrial processing.

Defines saffron as the dried portion of the pistils (stigmas with part of the style) of Crocus sativus L., describes stigma/style morphology, and permits sale in styles filaments, cut filaments, powdered, or other clearly labelled styles with brief definitions of each style.

Defines the product as dried floral parts of saffron (Crocus sativus L.), specifying that saffron is obtained from the pistil portion (stigmas with part of the style) and providing morphological definitions for 'stigma' and 'style'.

Lists permitted presentation styles—filaments, cut filaments, powdered—and allows other distinctly different styles if labelled; defines filament (dried stigma with part of style), cut filament (stigma with styles removed), and powdered (particles from crushed filaments).

Specifies composition as dried floral parts per Section 2 and requires characteristic odour, flavour and colour and compliance with chemical and physical criteria in Annex I, prohibits adulteration, requires saffron powder to be safe and practically free from insects and hazardous extraneous matter, and establishes classification into extra, grade I, II and III with ungraded products subject to grade III minimum requirements.
States the product composition is dried floral parts as described in Section 2 (composition requirement).

Requires the product to have characteristic odour/flavour/colour and to comply with chemical and physical requirements in Annex I, prohibits adulteration, and requires saffron powder to be safe for consumption and practically free from live insects and hazardous extraneous matter.

Requires saffron to have a characteristic odour, flavour and colour (which may vary by geo‑climatic conditions) and be free from foreign odours, flavours or colours, especially rancidity and mustiness.

Mandates compliance with the chemical and physical characteristics set out in Annex I, limits defects to those not affecting overall quality, forbids any adulteration, and requires saffron powder to be safe, free from living insects and practically free from extraneous/foreign matter that could pose a health hazard.

Provides classification based on the chemical and physical characteristics into extra, grade I, grade II and grade III, and states that unclassified/ungraded saffron must meet grade III minimum requirements.
Prohibits the use of any food additives in products covered by this standard.
Requires compliance with the Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex maximum residue limits for pesticides.

Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Low‑Moisture Foods (CXC 75-2015) including Annex III on spices and dried herbs, and requires compliance with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.
Requires containers to be filled as fully as practicable without impairing quality and to be consistent with an accurate declaration of contents.

Mandates labelling per the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) with additional provisions covering product name/style/variety, an item on country of origin/harvest to be developed, grade/class where applicable, and non‑retail labelling per CXS 346-2021.
Requires the product name to be 'dried saffron' (Section 2.1), allows the style to be indicated and permits listing of variety or cultivar on the label.

sections.section: 8.3 Country of origin and country of harvest
sections.summary: Placeholder section: country of origin and country of harvest labelling provisions are to be developed and contain no substantive requirements.
Commercial identification should include Grade/Class where applicable for the product.
Labelling of non-retail containers must comply with the Codex General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
For compliance checking, methods of analysis and sampling referenced in Codex CXS 234-1999 shall be used where relevant, while the sampling plan is noted as to be developed.

Methods of analysis and sampling for checking compliance with this standard are those contained in the Codex Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) as relevant to the standard's provisions.
Placeholder section: a sampling plan is to be developed and contains no substantive sampling requirements yet.
Non-substantive heading: Annex I (no content provided here).
Notes/bibliography listing referenced Codex standards and guidelines (e.g., CXS 193-1995, CXC 1-1969, CXC 75-2015, CXG 21-1997, CXS 1-1985, CXS 346-2021, CXS 234-1999) as sources for the standard.
